page.title: Tour Guide Via Baltica — Mark Jeziak, Gdańsk
hero.heading:
Discover Gdańsk
like a local
hero.body: Private guided tours through one of Europe's most beautiful Baltic cities — where history, amber and the sea come together.
about.heading:
A passion for stories,
a love for Gdańsk

about.h1desc: Certified by the Polish Tourist Organisation with 18+ years of experience.
about.h2desc: Tours conducted in English and Polish, tailored to your group's pace.
about.h3desc: Born and raised in Gdańsk — every hidden courtyard and legend known.
about.h4desc: Consistently top-rated on Google with glowing reviews from across the world.
trips.heading: Available Tours
trips.subheading: Choose a category, then swipe through the tours inside.

trips.title: Old Town & Waterfront
trips.body: Stroll through the Royal Road, marvel at the Neptune Fountain, and discover the stories behind Gdańsk's iconic crane on the Motława river — a city born of trade and amber.

trips.title: WWII History Tour
trips.body: Where WWII began — stand on the grounds of Westerplatte, explore the Museum of the Second World War, and hear the story of a city that rebuilt itself from rubble.

trips.title: Street Art & Shipyard
trips.body: The Gdańsk Shipyard — birthplace of Solidarity — is now a canvas for artists. Explore murals, the European Solidarity Centre, and the raw creative energy of the post-industrial zone.

trips.title: Craft Beer & Local Food
trips.body: Taste Gdańsk from the inside: local craft breweries, smoked fish markets, traditional Kashubian dishes, and the hidden courtyards of the Wrzeszcz district.

trips.title: Amber Route Sunrise Walk
trips.body: Begin at dawn in Oliwa Park, home to centuries-old oaks and a baroque cathedral. Learn the secrets of Baltic amber as morning light filters through the canopy.

trips.title: Sopot & Baltic Pier
trips.body: Walk Europe's longest wooden pier, breathe in Baltic sea air, and explore the elegant spa town that once charmed kaisers and artists alike.

trips.title: Gdynia Port & Modernism
trips.body: Explore Poland's inter-war modernist gem: the port city of Gdynia, built almost from scratch in the 1920s, with its Art Deco architecture and museum ships.

trips.title: Vistula Delta Birdwatching
trips.body: The Żuławy wetlands at the Vistula delta are a migratory paradise. Spot white-tailed eagles, rare waders, and vast reed beds stretching to the horizon.

trips.title: Malbork Castle Day Trip
trips.body: Visit the largest brick castle in the world. The mighty Teutonic stronghold on the Nogat river tells a story of power, faith, and medieval architecture on a grand scale.

trips.title: Kashubian Lake District
trips.body: Venture into the Kashubian highlands — rolling hills, glacial lakes, and a culture unlike any other in Poland. Meet local craftsmen and taste regional żurek.

reviews.heading: Trusted by travellers
reviews.r1.text: Mark is an exceptional guide — knowledgeable, passionate, and incredibly engaging. The Via Baltica tour exceeded every expectation. Highly recommended!
reviews.r2.text: Absolutely fantastic experience in Gdańsk. Mark brings history to life in a way no textbook ever could. We'll be back for the next tour!
reviews.r3.text: One of the best guided experiences I've had in Europe. The route along Via Baltica was perfectly curated, and Mark's storytelling made every stop memorable.
